expectations.text: Report file is saved as a markdown file at the specified output path
Report has an Options Analysis section that is more than 20 lines
expectations.evidence: Options Analysis section (lines 134-155) is 22 lines. Additionally, the report has a 'Put-Selling Decision Framework' section (lines 264-276, 13 lines) and 'What Would Invalidate This Thesis' (lines 278-283, 6 lines) that extend options analysis further.

expectations.text: Report mentions IV Rank or IV Percentile with numeric values in the options analysis
expectations.evidence: 'IV Rank: ~60-75% (estimated)' and 'Historical Median IV: 62.5% (per Market Chameleon data)' — estimated but numeric, not N/A

expectations.text: Report specifically evaluates the put-selling strategy the user asked about
expectations.evidence: Detailed put-selling analysis: March 27 $17P, April 17 $16P, April 17 $17P with premiums, breakevens, annualized yields

expectations.text: Report recommends for or against selling puts with reasoning tied to IV environment
expectations.evidence: 'SELL PUTS — IV elevated, fundamentals strong, insider buying provides conviction floor. Cash-secured puts at $16-$17 strike, April 17 expiry.'

expectations.text: Report contains a Recommendation section with explicit action
Report contains Conviction level (High, Medium, or Low)
Report has a Valuation Summary section with multiples
expectations.evidence: P/E trailing ~48x, forward 31.3x, P/S 6.7x, P/B 2.27x with peer comparisons

expectations.text: Report has fundamental analysis providing context for the options play
expectations.evidence: Extensive fundamental section: Q4 revenue $1.013B, EBITDA $318M, member growth 13.7M, loan originations $10.5B, revenue diversification analysis

expectations.text: Report has a Sources section with references
Report contains actual current SOFI price data (not fabricated)
Report presents multiple named options strategies or strike/expiry alternatives
expectations.evidence: Multiple strategies: March 27 $17P ($0.97), April 17 $16P ($0.50-0.70), April 17 $17P ($0.80-1.10), plus Jan 2027 $50 calls as alternative

expectations.text: Report includes risk factors or invalidation criteria specific to the options position
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'What Would Invalidate This Thesis' section with 5 specific conditions: credit deterioration, member growth <20%, NIM <4.5%, management changes, macro recession. Plus 'Put-Selling Decision Framework' table.
